name: Architecture View
description: VS Code WebviewView sidebar panel ("Architecture") that renders the architecture wiki as a searchable list of component cards. Hosts the shared @code-workbench/ui ArchPanel React component and answers its RPC calls (list/upsert/remove/openCard/search) against the repo's .code-workbench/.arch/<slug>.json cards — the SAME store the bundled arch MCP server (arch-server.mjs) reads and writes, so cards Claude authors via seed-arch/arch_upsert and cards edited in the panel are one and the same files. Clicking a card opens its <slug>.json file in the normal editor (openCard RPC → showTextDocument); there is no in-webview graph, card-detail, or editor form. The RPC handler set is built by buildArchRpcHandlers(ctx, getRepoRoot) exported from archView.ts and shared with the full-page board (archPage.ts). The .arch FileSystemWatcher is owned by ArchViewProvider itself (created in the constructor and re-pointed in onRepoChanged) — NOT by the webview lifecycle — so external card writes refresh both the sidebar and the full page even in a window where the sidebar view was never resolved; onCardsChanged (wired to refreshArchPage) fans the watcher out to the page. The page does not re-push 'arch-changed' on reveal/visible — the watcher is the only change signal, which keeps the panel from re-running semantic search for identical results. Originally ported from the removed Electron app's ArchWikiPane; the force-directed ArchGraph and the in-webview CardEditor form were later removed.
guidelines: RPC handlers live in buildArchRpcHandlers(ctx, getRepoRoot) in archView.ts — reuse it in both the sidebar provider and archPage; do not duplicate the list/upsert/remove/openCard/search bodies.
The full-width board is codeWorkbench.arch.openAsPage (archPage.ts showArchPage), mirroring codeWorkbench.tasks.openAsPage; it reuses the same webview/arch.tsx bundle.
ArchPanel semantic search is gated on api.search being wired; the sidebar and page both pass it, so semantic ranking is available in both surfaces.
Keep the .arch watcher's lifetime tied to the provider/extension, not to resolveWebviewView — the full-page board depends on it even when the sidebar was never opened.
anti_patterns: Don't re-sort the card list in ListView when semantic ranking is active — pass preserveOrder so the embedding order survives.

Don't treat an empty api.search result as an error — [] means the model is unavailable OR no card cleared the relevance floor; the panel falls back to substring filtering (which may legitimately show the empty state).
Don't post 'arch-changed' from page reveal/visible hooks — it re-runs the panel's semantic search for identical results; the file watcher is the only change signal.
decisions: The Architecture panel was ported from the removed Electron app's ArchWikiPane into the shared ui package so it renders identically; the Electron useArchWiki/window.api hook was replaced by an injected ArchApi adapter wired over the webview postMessage RPC bridge (webviewRpc.ts / bridge.ts).
The force-directed ArchGraph nodes view was removed; the panel is now a search + list only.

The in-webview CardEditor form was removed (commit f04702d, feat: add openCard). Cards are now edited by opening their <slug>.json file in the normal editor via the openCard RPC; the old openFile RPC and the read-only detail pane were dropped with it.
Live refresh uses a VS Code FileSystemWatcher instead of the Electron app's chokidar + coalesce watcher; event debouncing is delegated to VS Code's watcher.
The watcher moved from resolveWebviewView into the ArchViewProvider constructor so the full-page board refreshes without the sidebar ever being resolved (review fix on afba156).
